title: Java Arrays
content:
### Arrays in Java

An array is a container object that holds a fixed number of values of a single type.

---

### Declaring Arrays

```java
int[] numbers = new int[5];         // array of 5 integers
int[] numbers = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5};    // array literal
int[][] matrix = new int[3][3];      // 2D array
```

---

### Array Properties

| Property | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| Fixed size | Cannot change after creation |
| Zero-indexed | First element at index 0 |
| Homogeneous | All elements same type |
| length field | `array.length` gives size |

---

### Accessing Elements

```java
int[] arr = {10, 20, 30, 40, 50};
arr[0] = 100;              // assign value
int x = arr[2];            // read value
int len = arr.length;      // array length
```

---

### Iterating Arrays

```java
// for loop
for (int i = 0; i < arr.length; i++) {
    System.out.println(arr[i]);
}

// enhanced for-each
for (int num : arr) {
    System.out.println(num);
}
```

---

### Multidimensional Arrays

```java
int[][] grid = {
    {1, 2, 3},
    {4, 5, 6},
    {7, 8, 9}
};
int val = grid[1][2];  // 6
```

> **Key Point:** Arrays have a fixed length. Use `Arrays.toString()` for printing and `Arrays.sort()` for sorting. The `java.util.Arrays` class provides many utility methods.

quiz.question: What is the index of the first element in an array?
Can an array's size be changed after creation?
